by_judge.judge: K V ARAVIND WILL SIT IN COURT HALL NO.4 AT KALABURAGI
C M POONACHA WILL SIT IN COURT HALL NO.6 AT DHARWAD
SACHIN SHANKAR MAGADUM WILL SIT IN COURT HALL NO.4 AT
D K SINGH & VENKATESH NAIK T
D K SINGH & S VISHWAJITH SHETTY
D K SINGH & VIJAYKUMAR A. PATIL
R NATARAJ IS NOT HOLDING SITTING ON 17.07.2026

narrative:
On July 17, 2026, the Karnataka High Court achieved an overall hearing rate of 49.2%, resolving 1,037 cases out of 2,108 scheduled across 59 judges. This performance reflects a steady upward trajectory compared to earlier periods, improving from the 45.4% hearing rate recorded on July 10 (926 of 2,038 cases heard) and the 45.9% rate observed on June 25 (1,045 of 2,275 cases).

Individual judge workloads were dominated by Justice S. Vishwajith Shetty in Court Hall 19, who heard 97 of his 203 scheduled cases (47.8%). Justice M. Nagaprasanna also managed a substantial caseload across two sessions in Court Hall 14, collectively hearing 129 of 265 scheduled matters. High conversion rates were notable for Justice Suraj Govindaraj, who successfully addressed 87.8% of 82 scheduled cases in Court Hall 10. Conversely, Justice B.M. Shyam Prasad cleared only 10 of his 107 primary scheduled cases, resulting in a 9.3% hearing rate for that block.

Procedurally, Orders represented the most active stage with 665 scheduled matters and a 53.2% clearance rate. Preliminary Hearings demonstrated a high conversion rate of 61.9% (125 of 202 cases), while Final Hearings lagged behind at 35.0% (112 of 320 cases). By case type, Writ Petitions (WP) constituted the bulk of court activity with 760 scheduled cases and a 45.1% clearance rate. Miscellaneous First Appeals (MFA) and Criminal Petitions (CRL.P) also showed strong progression, achieving conversion rates of 51.9% (191 of 368 cases) and 48.7% (163 of 335 cases), respectively.
